message:
feat(webgpu): persistent uniform buffer, surface error classification, and render init cleanup

Replace per-frame GPU buffer+bind-group allocation with a persistent
shader uniform buffer that is updated via queue.write_buffer(), reducing
per-frame allocations and GPU object churn.

- Add `shader_uniform_buffer` and `shader_uniform_bind_group` fields to
  WebGpuState, created once at initialization
- Replace `create_uniform()` with `update_uniform()` (write to existing
  buffer) and `shader_uniform_bind_group()` accessor
- draw.rs: switch from create_uniform + bind_group to update_uniform +
  shader_uniform_bind_group()
- Change `select_surface_format()` from panicking to returning Result,
  add test for empty format list
- Refactor `opengl_initialize()` to use `?` error propagation instead
  of match-and-panic pattern
- Extract `classify_webgpu_surface_error()` for exhaustive handling of
  wgpu::SurfaceError variants: Retry (Lost/Outdated), SkipFrame
  (Timeout), Fail (OutOfMemory/Other)
- Handle Timeout by invalidating the window for a repaint instead of
  propagating the error
- Add unit tests for all surface error classification branches

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
